---
title: "4.17 Recursive Searching and Sorting"
subtitle: "Part 2: Merge Sort"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit4-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Algorithm Idea

### Divide, sort, merge

<span class="term">Merge sort</span> is a recursive sorting algorithm.

Steps:

::: {.tight-list}
- split the list into two halves
- recursively sort each half
- merge the two sorted halves
:::

Base case: a list of length 0 or 1 is already sorted.

## Split Trace

### Smaller and smaller lists

For:

```text
[8, 3, 5, 1]
```

Split:

```text
[8, 3] and [5, 1]
[8] [3] [5] [1]
```

Then merge sorted pieces back together.

## Merge Step

### Choose the smaller front value

Merging `[3, 8]` and `[1, 5]`:

| left front | right front | output |
|---:|---:|---|
| 3 | 1 | `[1]` |
| 3 | 5 | `[1, 3]` |
| 8 | 5 | `[1, 3, 5]` |
| 8 | empty | `[1, 3, 5, 8]` |

## Merge Sort Source Code {.fit-small}

### Recursive structure

```java
public static void mergeSort(int[] values, int low, int high)
{
    if (low < high)
    {
        int mid = (low + high) / 2;
        mergeSort(values, low, mid);
        mergeSort(values, mid + 1, high);
        merge(values, low, mid, high);
    }
}
```

The helper `merge` combines two sorted subarrays.

## Tracing Challenge

### Recursive search and sort

Group tasks:

::: {.tight-list}
- trace recursive binary search for one found target and one missing target
- trace merge sort splits for a four-element array
- trace the final merge step
- identify the base case in both algorithms
:::

## Classroom Check

### A strong answer should include

::: {.tight-list}
- explain merge sort as divide, sort, and merge
- identify the base case for a subarray of size 0 or 1
- trace recursive splits before merge steps
- explain what the merge helper does
- compare recursive binary search and merge sort as smaller-range algorithms
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
